name: Vitamin E (Tocopherol)
description: A fat-soluble antioxidant that protects oil-based formulations from oxidative rancidity by terminating lipid peroxidation chain reactions — the essential preservative for any DIY product containing unsaturated oils.
appearance: Pale yellow to amber viscous oil
solubility: Insoluble in water; soluble in oils, fats, ethanol
chemistry: Chain-breaking antioxidant. Donates phenolic hydrogen to lipid peroxyl radicals (LOO•), terminating oxidation chain. Gamma-tocopherol > alpha-tocopherol for formulation antioxidant potency. Use at 0.5–1% in oil phase.
warnings: Not a standalone preservative — prevents lipid oxidation only, NOT microbial growth. Contact dermatitis in ~3–5% of individuals. Degrades with air/light/heat exposure. Scar treatment evidence is weak.
shelfLife: 2–3 years unopened; 1 year after opening. Store in dark glass.
